insight: Most of this jump is the IVT plus the safety suite — ADAS, 360° camera and ventilated seats are daily-use gains, so it's expensive but not hollow.
The classic sweet-spot upgrade: the bigger screen, pano roof carry-over and 17-inch wheels make the SX feel like a different car for a modest step.
Nearly the same money buys 45 PS more and all-disc brakes instead of comfort kit — pick by whether your commute is traffic or highway.
This step buys the cabin you saw in the ads — pano roof, big screens and leatherette — and is where the Seltos starts feeling premium.
A big cheque, but it covers the turbo engine, DCT, ADAS and 360° camera together — the only Seltos that fully outruns the Creta.
Zeta adds the sunroof, screen and connected tech most buyers actually wanted — the Delta is fleet-spec by comparison.
The hybrid premium looks steep until you run the fuel math — at city mileages above ~12,000 km/year it pays itself back well before resale.
Same trim, two powertrains: the hybrid costs more upfront but turns every fuel stop in its favour — the spreadsheet pick for high-km users.
VX is where Honda Sensing ADAS and blind-spot view land — the rare mid-trim where the gain is mostly safety, not garnish.
Ventilated seats and the digital cluster are nice, but the Highline already has the bones — pay up only if you sit in traffic daily.
This is an engine upgrade dressed as a trim: the 1.5 TSI with DSG transforms the car, and the feature delta is almost incidental.
The Signature+ packs the comfort kit — sunroof, ventilated seats, bigger alloys — making it the value-per-rupee peak of the Kushaq range.
Accomplished stacks genuinely high-impact kit — ventilated seats, 360° camera, powered driver seat — so the step reads expensive but scores well.
AX7 brings the stronger TGDi engine plus 360° camera and disc brakes all round — one of the best single-step upgrades in this entire comparison.
Sharp Pro adds the pano roof, ventilated seats and powered chair that justify the Astor's premium pitch; below this it's a feature-list car without the features.
